pdg_node.statement: (lf: Leapfrog/state-transition context, r: Real scalar, step: Integer, n_steps: Integer) -> Real scalar
pdg_node.informal_desc: Computes a deterministic tempering multiplier across sub-steps (with bounds checking) to scale the transition strength.

declaration.name: temperingfactorcomputation
declaration.type_signature: (lf: Leapfrog/state-transition context, r: Real scalar, step: Integer, n_steps: Integer) -> Real scalar
declaration.docstring: Computes a deterministic tempering multiplier across sub-steps (with bounds checking) to scale the transition strength.
declaration.conceptual_summary: IndexedTransitionGainSchedule. Maps a global intensity parameter and a discrete sub-step index to a deterministic scalar gain used to modulate update magnitude within a multi-step process. The computation enforces index validity against the total step count, yielding a well-defined scaling factor for every admissible sub-step. This is a pure schedule evaluation: no persistent state is mutated, and output is fully determined by the provided inputs.. Applications: Adaptive learning-rate scaling across inner iterations of numerical optimization in scientific computing, Stepwise trust-region or damping control in nonlinear equation solvers for computational physics, Scheduled actuation gain modulation across micro-steps in robotic trajectory execution, Progressive regularization-strength scheduling over sub-epochs in neural network training

pdg_node.statement: (lf: DefaultLeapfrog context, h: Hamiltonian, loop_state: {i, theta, r, value, gradient, z_current}, step_count: Int, epsilon: Step-size scalar/vector) -> {i, theta_next, r_next, value_next, gradient_next, z_candidate}
pdg_node.informal_desc: Execute one leapfrog proposal: pre-temper momentum, half momentum update, full position update via dH/dr, second half momentum update via dH/dtheta, post-temper, then build a cached phase-point candidate.

pdg_node.statement: (candidate_state: {i, theta_next, r_next, value_next, gradient_next, z_candidate}, trajectory_accum: Nothing | Vector{P}, collect_full_trajectory: Bool, step_count: Int) -> tuple[{i, theta, r, value, gradient, z_current}, Nothing | Vector{P}, Bool, Bool]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Optionally append candidate to trajectory, evaluate finiteness, truncate realized trajectory on first invalid sample, and compute next loop state plus continuation decision.
